task.title: Migrate public coordination API to session queue scoped leases
task.summary: Replace public claim vocabulary with artifact-status-first session queue coordination while preserving transitional compatibility for existing claim/lease callers.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki public coordination language/API expose artifact availability, in-use, waiting, conflict, and stale status from session queue state; sessions are holders/waiters behind artifact status, and /wiki-resume allocates safe parallel work from that state.
acceptance: Domain/session queue types model artifact statuses for tasks, paths, builds, validation refs, generated state, and publication refs with holders, waiters, heartbeat/TTL, handoff metadata, and isolation context.
Public API/tool/UI/graph/prompt language uses artifact status terminology (available, in-use, waiting, conflict, stale, holder, waiter) instead of claim/lease as primary concepts.
Legacy codewiki_claim and any lease/claim naming remains only as compatibility/internal implementation detail with deprecation/migration notes if needed.
New or renamed application tool/API surface exposes artifact-status operations: inspect status, mark/use artifact for read/write work, wait, heartbeat, release, and list holders/waiters.
Storage remains .codewiki/session/queue.json unless migration planning explicitly changes it.
Tests cover artifact status create/list/release/heartbeat/wait behavior and legacy alias compatibility.

/wiki-resume without an explicit task reads fresh CodeWiki state plus session queue artifact statuses and selects the next unblocked atomic task whose relevant artifacts are available or read-compatible.
/wiki-resume TASK-### honors an explicit non-closed task request even when an umbrella task is active, unless task policy or artifact conflict provides a real block with a clear explanation.

Resume output includes selected task, artifact statuses, conflicting artifacts and holders/waiters, temporary session usage record, and helper-safe next steps so one tab/session can start parallel work with a single /wiki-resume invocation.
non_goals: Do not break existing users abruptly by deleting codewiki_claim before compatibility alias and migration notes exist.
Do not treat session queue artifact status as durable roadmap truth.
Do not use broad umbrella tasks such as TASK-077 as default implementation work when safe child/atomic tasks are available.
Do not make claim or lease primary project lexicon terms.
verification: targeted legacy claim/lease alias compatibility smoke
targeted /wiki-resume parallel allocation smoke with active umbrella focus and artifact conflict fixtures
stale-term scan for public claim/lease vocabulary with allowed compatibility/internal exceptions only

loop_start.reason: Compiler loops start from source refs and build handoffs, not prior loop chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Implementation validation must not reuse builder thought context and must cite checked content proof.

next_loop.reason: The next gateway must independently validate implementation evidence and cite checked content proof.

summary: Implemented TASK-086 second slice: public codewiki_artifact_status tool/API, artifact statuses in graph/state, and legacy codewiki_claim alias language moved to compatibility.

evidence: Extended package smoke to exercise codewiki_artifact_status mark/release and state artifact_statuses while keeping legacy codewiki_claim compatibility checks.
Existing resume scheduler smoke continues to cover /wiki-resume artifact-status allocation.
tester.boundary: derive tests or test-design evidence before code changes where practical

evidence: Added public artifact status Pi tool wrapper over session queue runtime storage.
Added artifact status action schema/input types and graph/status state projections.
Updated public README/skill surfaces and codewiki_state summary wording toward artifact status.
notes: This does not rename historical storage keys or internal compatibility types in one step; it establishes primary public artifact-status surface and keeps codewiki_claim compatibility.
builder.boundary: change code until tests, roadmap acceptance, and required checks pass

test_design_evidence: Extended package smoke to exercise codewiki_artifact_status mark/release and state artifact_statuses while keeping legacy codewiki_claim compatibility checks.
Existing resume scheduler smoke continues to cover /wiki-resume artifact-status allocation.
code_change_evidence: Added public artifact status Pi tool wrapper over session queue runtime storage.
Added artifact status action schema/input types and graph/status state projections.
Updated public README/skill surfaces and codewiki_state summary wording toward artifact status.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Public API/tool language uses artifact status as primary coordination concept while keeping legacy claim alias.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: src/adapters/pi/tools/artifact-status.ts registers codewiki_artifact_status; src/adapters/pi/index.ts marks codewiki_claim as legacy compatibility alias; README.md and skills/codewiki/SKILL.md list artifact status as primary tool.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: New application/tool surface exposes artifact-status operations to inspect/mark/wait/heartbeat/release/list holders and waiters.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: CodewikiArtifactStatusToolInput action values cover mark, wait, release, heartbeat, list; mutateArtifactStatuses maps to session queue storage and returns artifact_statuses with holders/waiters/conflicts.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Session queue state exposes artifact statuses from existing runtime records.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: buildChangeClaimState now derives artifact_statuses; graph/status state expose artifact_status/artifact_statuses; codewiki_state summary says Artifacts in-use/warnings/conflicts.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Tests cover public artifact-status tool and legacy alias compatibility.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: package-smoke asserts codewiki_artifact_status registration, mark/release behavior, artifact-status output language, artifact_statuses in state, and existing codewiki_claim legacy behavior.

closure_brief.user_intent: Move beyond claim/lease vocabulary toward artifact-status-first public coordination.
implemented_changes: Exposed artifact_statuses through session queue state, graph, and codewiki_state details.
Updated /wiki-resume scheduler slice to coexist with public artifact-status tool.
Marked codewiki_claim as legacy alias and kept compatibility tested.
acceptance_evidence: legacy codewiki_claim still allocates CLAIM ids and conflict behavior
non_goals_preserved: No storage path migration away from .codewiki/session/queue.json.
No skill-owned resume template migration; TASK-083 owns that.
remaining_risks: Full public stale-term scan/cleanup can run before closing TASK-086, or be deferred if compatibility wording is accepted.

risks: Some internal type names and historical graph node kinds still mention claim for compatibility and migration; stale-term tightening may remain before final TASK-086 closure.

handoff.fallback: Use codewiki_state refresh=true and this implementation_build; do not rely on chat transcript memory.

Implemented TASK-086 second slice: public codewiki_artifact_status tool/API, artifact statuses in graph/state, and legacy codewiki_claim alias language moved to compatibility.

Checks: npm run typecheck, npm run test:smoke, npm run check:architecture, npm test
Validation: no durable validation refs recorded.
Remote publication requires explicit approval; this build is recommendation-only.
